***![][image1]The Note & Deed of Trust Template***

**Purchase Price**

* Purchase Price Shall be $\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_.

**Down Payment & Earnest Money**

* Down payment shall be $\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Inclusive of $\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ earnest money. 

**Promissory Note** 

* Buyer (Maker) Shall give Seller (Beneficiary) a Promissory Note in the amount of $\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_.

**Interest Rate / Accrual**

* Promissory Note shall bear interest at \_\_\_\_\_\_\_% simple interest per annum.   
  * *There can be a lot of details here based on your negotiation.*   
* Interest shall begin accruing \_\_\_\_\_\_.

**Payments**

* First payment shall be due \_\_\_\_\_\_ months from closing and each consecutive month thereafter.   
* Payments shall be interest only or more. (or “payments shall be $\_\_\_\_, or more” if your seller is insisting on a particular minimum amount per month.)  
* All outstanding principal balance due beneficiary on or before \_\_\_\_\_\_ years from closing.   
* *First Right of Refusal Clause***:** Maker of Note has First Right of Refusal to Purchase note should beneficiary elect to sell note prior to maturity.   
* Co beneficiaries on the note shall be \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_. (if applicable).   
* Remainder Beneficiaries of the note shall be \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_. (if applicable).

**Collateral**

* Promissory Note shall be initially collateralized by a deed of trust on subject property, commonly known as \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_, Legally described as \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_.


*Substitution of Security Clause:* 

 Maker, at Makers option, may substitute the collateral of the note to a different piece of real property under the following two conditions.

1. The substitute property has equity equal to or greater than the outstanding principal balance of the note at the time of substitution.

2. The net positive cash flow in the substitute collateral is equal to or greater than the minimum committed payment on the note at the time of substitution.

   * This is where you would insert a slice and dice clause if you intend to use it. Typically, you will use this if the amount of the note is fairly large.   
- *Slice and Dice Clause (Exchange of Note Clause***).** The Note may be exchanged into multiple notes of smaller amounts, provided that (i) the aggregate sum of the smaller notes equals the outstanding principle balance on the Note at the time of the exchange, (ii) the aggregate sum of monthly payments from the smaller notes equals the minimum committed monthly payment on the Note at the time of the exchange, **and** (iii) all of the provisions set forth herein shall apply to such smaller notes including, without limitation, this Exchange of Note clause, the Right of First Refusal clause and the Substitution of Collateral clause.


  **Attachments**


  Attached are blank Promissory Note Form Number: \_\_\_\_\_\_ and Blank Deed of Trust Form Number: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ which shall be prepared in escrow according to the terms and conditions set forth herein. 

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
